domain.title: Ground-based Gravitational Waves (LVK)
domain.band: audio-band gravitational waves (10 Hz – few kHz)

frames: source-frame vs detector-frame masses (redshifted): report source-frame unless asked
GPS time; GWOSC event naming GWYYMMDD_hhmmss with a version vN
numbered GWTC catalogs are per-observing-run SEGMENTS; only the umbrella GWTC is cumulative
chirp mass is the tightest-measured mass combination; individual masses are broader
conventions.scope_trap: A superlative within a numbered GWTC-N release is NOT the cumulative answer: GWTC-N is one run, not the whole catalog. Resolve every 'so far / ever / most / largest' against the umbrella GWTC catalog, and report both the cumulative and the named-release answer. (Canonical: GW231123_135430 ~236 M_sun is the cumulative most-massive but sits in GWTC-4.1/O4a, NOT GWTC-5.0.)

skeptic_threats: waveform-model systematics (precession, higher modes, tidal terms for NS)
prior-dominated marginals (individual masses, spin tilt, inclination) vs well-measured (chirp mass)
selection effects: the detected population is not the astrophysical population
calibration uncertainty and detector glitches / non-Gaussian noise
single-detector localization is poor; network antenna patterns matter
source-frame vs detector-frame mass confusion; cosmology assumption in redshift

speculative: electromagnetic counterpart association without a confirmed transient

open_limits: individual component masses and spins are often prior-dominated
formation channels are not resolved from single-event parameters
the detected population requires selection-effect correction before astrophysical inference
bridges: cosmology: standard-siren H0 from BNS/dark-siren distances
nuclear physics: neutron-star equation of state from tidal deformability
fundamental physics: tests of GR, graviton mass, dispersion
multimessenger: EM/neutrino follow-up; PTA/LISA complementarity across the GW spectrum
